name: Adler Law Firm: Chapter 7 & 13 Bankruptcy
description_short: Detroit-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, offering affordable legal representation starting at $499 with free consultations.
description_long:
Adler Law Firm PLLC is a bankruptcy law practice based in the Detroit, Michigan metro area that focuses on personal bankruptcy representation. The firm has filed over 1,100 successful bankruptcies and positions itself as an accessible option for financially distressed consumers seeking legal guidance through the bankruptcy process. The firm emphasizes affordability and stress reduction, marketing services starting at $499 and offering free initial consultations to evaluate clients' situations.

The firm handles both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings. Chapter 7 services focus on debt elimination for unsecured debts including credit card debt, medical bills, payday loans, and utility bills, typically completed within 3-6 months. Chapter 13 services involve reorganization and repayment plan setup, typically lasting 3-5 years. They also address specific collection issues including wage garnishment stops, foreclosure prevention, repossession halts, and creditor harassment cessation through automatic stay injunctions.

Adler Law Firm differentiates itself primarily through pricing ($499 entry point) and accessibility, positioning the service as affordable relative to typical bankruptcy attorney rates. The firm emphasizes the speed and straightforwardness of their process and provides educational content through a Learning Center to help clients understand bankruptcy law and their protections. Their marketing highlights the free consultation model and attempts to demystify a complex legal process for stressed consumers.

The firm's primary limitation is that the website provides no verifiable credentials, attorney qualifications, client reviews, or performance metrics beyond the stated '1,100+ successful bankruptcies' figure, which cannot be independently verified from the provided content. The practice appears to be a volume-oriented firm emphasizing affordability over specialization, and potential clients should independently verify attorney licensing and credentials with the Michigan State Bar.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Low flat fee entry point ($499) compared to typical bankruptcy attorney rates, improving access for low-income filers
Free initial consultation to evaluate which bankruptcy chapter best serves the client's situation
Claims 1,100+ successful bankruptcies filed, suggesting operational experience with high filing volume
Addresses specific urgent collection issues: stops wage garnishments, halts foreclosures, prevents repossession, and stops creditor harassment
Explains 90-day window for recovering garnished funds, demonstrating knowledge of time-sensitive procedural requirements
Provides educational Learning Center resources to help clients understand bankruptcy law and legal protections
Offers online payment option and phone consultation scheduling for accessibility
cons: Website provides no attorney credentials, bar certifications, disciplinary history, or independently verifiable qualifications for the legal team
No client testimonials, case results, or third-party reviews available to validate service quality or outcomes
The '1,100+ successful bankruptcies' claim is unverified and cannot be confirmed from the website; no timeframe or methodology provided
Pricing structure unclear beyond $499 entry point—total fees, payment plans, add-on costs, and what is/isn't included not explicitly detailed
Limited information about Chapter 13 repayment plan specifics, creditor negotiations, or how the firm handles complex situations
best_for: Low-income Detroit metro residents with $5K–$50K+ in unsecured debt seeking affordable legal representation for Chapter 7 liquidation
Homeowners facing active foreclosure auctions who need immediate automatic stay relief and Chapter 13 repayment plan alternatives
Wage-garnished employees needing urgent legal action to stop employer garnishments and recover recent garnished funds within 90-day windows
Consumers overwhelmed by medical bills, credit card debt, and collection harassment seeking stress-free guidance through the bankruptcy process
diagnosis: Adler Law Firm is best suited for financially distressed Detroit-area residents with moderate to high unsecured debt who prioritize affordability and accessibility over attorney credentials or verified track records. The primary caveat is the complete absence of verifiable attorney qualifications, client reviews, disciplinary history, or independently confirmed case outcomes—clients must independently verify licensing and credentials with the Michigan State Bar before engaging services.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing and representation (debt elimination, typically 3-6 months)
Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing and representation (repayment plan setup, typically 3-5 years)
Free initial bankruptcy consultation and chapter evaluation
Automatic stay injunction processing to stop creditor collection actions
Home foreclosure prevention and mortgage payment catch-up assistance
Vehicle and property repossession prevention and reversal (in applicable situations)
Creditor harassment and debt collection cessation support
Debt discharge evaluation for dischargeable vs. non-dischargeable debts
Educational resources and Learning Center guidance
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 17000 W 10 Mile Rd Suite 201, Southfield, MI 48075
